name: Segura de la Sierra and Orcera MTB Loop
name_original: Segura de la Sierra - circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
description: This moderate 14.0-mile mountain biking route in Sierras de Cazorla Natural Park offers diverse terrain and stunning views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Segura De La Sierra offers diverse terrain within the vast Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The region features extensive pine forests, mountainous areas with significant elevation changes, and river valleys carved by the Segura and Madera rivers. Riders can expect routes that traverse varied topography, including challenging ascents and technical descents, with peaks like El Yelmo providing panoramic views. Best mountain bike trails around Segura De La Sierra The most popular mountain bike trail is Segura de la Sierra and Orcera MTB Loop, a 14.0 miles (22.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses varied topography within the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Río Madera Inn and Lodging loop from Camping Garrote Gordo, a difficult 32.9 miles (53.0 km) path. This route winds through extensive pine forests and along river valleys. Local mountain bikers also love the Castle of Segura de la Sierra – Segura de la Sierra loop from Cementerio Municipal, a 37.4 miles (60.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with views of the historic castle, often completed in about 6 hours 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Segura De La Sierra is defined by vast pine forests, mountainous areas with significant elevation, and clear river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Segura De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Segura De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Segura de la Sierra?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Segura de la Sierra, offering a variety of experiences within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Segura de la Sierra?
A: You can expect diverse terrain, ranging from extensive pine forests and river valleys to mountainous areas with significant elevation changes. Routes often feature challenging ascents with gradients up to 15% and technical descents, providing a varied experience for mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes in Segura de la Sierra are moderate to difficult, there are options that cater to varying skill levels. For a moderate experience that traverses varied topography, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993776436/segura-de-la-sierra-and-orcera-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura de la Sierra and Orcera MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.0 miles (22.5 km) long.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Segura de la Sierra – Segura de la Sierra loop from Cementerio Municipal&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.4 miles (60.1 km) trail with over 2,000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38144913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura de la Sierra – Peñalta de Benatae (1,412 m) loop from Segura de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, covering 83.5 miles (83.5 km) with significant climbing.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Segura de la Sierra are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993776436/segura-de-la-sierra-and-orcera-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura de la Sierra and Orcera MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33712981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Río Madera Inn and Lodging loop from Camping Garrote Gordo&lt;/a&gt; are both popular circular options.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2064086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sagra Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despiernacaballos Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Segura de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning views from its elevated position.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting on a mountain bike ride?
A: Absolutely. The summit of El Yelmo (1809m) offers incredible panoramic views of the entire Natural Park, La Mancha, and even the foothills of the Sierra Nevada. The Mirador de Jorge Manrique also provides extensive views of the Natural Park. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38215006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo Summit (1,808 m) – El Robledo Viewpoint loop from Cementerio Municipal&lt;/a&gt; for a route that includes these vistas.

Q: Is Segura de la Sierra suitable for family mountain biking?
A: While many trails feature significant elevation changes and technical sections, some moderate routes might be suitable for families with older, experienced children. It's best to check the difficulty and elevation profiles of specific routes to ensure they match your family's skill level.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Segura de la Sierra?
A: The region experiences diverse weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, while winters can bring cold temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, especially around peaks like El Yelmo.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Natural Park?
A: Mountain biking is generally permitted on designated trails within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to check with local authorities or the park's official website for any specific regulations or permit requirements, especially for certain protected areas or during specific seasons.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Segura de la Sierra?
A: The mountain bike trails in Segura de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the stunning panoramic views, the diverse terrain, and the opportunity to experience wild nature within the vast Natural Park.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park is home to rich biodiversity. You might spot Spanish ibex, deer, wild boar, and mouflon. The cliffs and gorges around Cumbre de Segura la Vieja are also known as breeding sites for vultures.
tips: Segura de la Sierra is a Spanish town and municipality in the province of Jaén, in the autonomous community of Andalusia, belonging to the region of Sierra de Segura, located northeast of Andalusia. According to INE sources, in 2017 it had 1,807 inhabitants. Its municipal area, with an area of 225.02 km², is the most disintegrated in the province, having four exclaves apart from the main nucleus. The five enclaves are within the Natural Park of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas and encompass a total of 18 population centers, 13 of them populated, with the Cortijos Nuevos district being the largest.
This section is a path that navigates the side of the mountain with views of Orcera.

faqs.question: How long does this mountain bike route typically take?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is approximately 22.5 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 9 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While it offers a rewarding experience, it's best suited for riders with a good fitness level and advanced riding skills. Some sections may be technical or steep, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: Where does this circular mountain bike route start?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in the historic town of Segura de la Sierra. You can find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient access point for the trail.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, ranging from forest tracks and smoother sections to more rugged paths. You can expect a mix of soil, rocky areas, and potentially some dry, loose, or shaly sections. There are also steep ascents and descents characteristic of the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning natural landscapes of pine forests and olive groves, you'll pass through the charming town of Segura de la Sierra. The route also features a Trail with views of Orcera, offering picturesque vistas of the surrounding region.

faqs.question: What wildlife and plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve rich in biodiversity. You might spot mountain goats, deer, wild boars, and various bird species, including eagles. The flora is equally diverse, with extensive pine forests and over 2,000 plant species, many unique to the area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures or even snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the Segura de la Sierra - Moralejos, Variante 1 (2/2), El Pilarillo - Peñalta, Camino de Orcera, Vía Pecuaria, Camino de Linarejos, Camino de Segura, and Peñalta trails.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this route is within the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local regulations before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain bike route or the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas. However, always respect park regulations, especially concerning waste disposal and wildlife.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's essential to bring appropriate mountain biking gear, including a helmet, gloves, and sturdy shoes. Carry plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, a first-aid kit, and navigation tools. Sun protection is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route starts and ends in Segura de la Sierra, where you can find cafes and restaurants to refuel before or after your ride. It's advisable to carry enough supplies for the duration of the ride itself, as options on the trail are limited.
